1. infra/scripts/deploy-shared.sh

This script:

* verifies Azure authentication;
* creates the shared resource group when missing;
* validates the shared template;
* runs what-if;
* creates the ACR;
* prints deployment outputs.


``` chmod +x infra/scripts/deploy-shared.sh ```

Run it 

``` ./infra/scripts/deploy-shared.sh ```

After deployment, infra/.outputs/shared.env will contain something like:


```
    # Generated by deploy-shared.sh
    # Generated at 2026-07-21T03:22:03Z
    # Do not edit manually.

    export SHARED_RESOURCE_GROUP=realty-shared-rg
    export SHARED_DEPLOYMENT_NAME=realty-shared
    export ACR_ID=/subscriptions/d27c4a16-2f78-43ba-9f1e-c36093d62a73/resourceGroups/realty-shared-rg/providers/Microsoft.ContainerRegistry/registries/realtydevacr
    export ACR_NAME=realtydevacr
    export ACR_LOGIN_SERVER=realtydevacr.azurecr.io
```
Load it with 

``` source infra/.outputs/shared.env ```

then verify 

``` 
printf '%s\n' "$ACR_NAME"
printf '%s\n' "$ACR_LOGIN_SERVER"
```

2. infra/scripts/deploy-environment.sh

This script:

* accepts dev or prod;
* resolves the matching parameter file;
* finds the ACR from the shared deployment outputs;
* builds an linux/amd64 backend image;
* tags the image with a Git commit SHA by default;
* pushes it to ACR;
* creates the environment resource group;
* validates and deploys the environment template;
* optionally starts the Ollama bootstrap and migration jobs.

``` chmod +x infra/scripts/deploy-environment.sh ```

Run dev

``` ./infra/scripts/deploy-environment.sh dev```

(Azure recommends Microsoft Entra authentication for ACR rather than enabling the registry admin user; az acr login uses the currently authenticated Azure identity. ???)

Make sure the output param names all match 

In case you want to skip the image build , give the corrent image tag 
``` SKIP_IMAGE_BUILD=true \
BACKEND_IMAGE="realtydevacr.azurecr.io/realty-backend:3ff10a1104f4" \
./infra/scripts/deploy-environment.sh dev ```
